rationale: Article VIII §1-g addresses tax incentives for reinvestment zones and bond issuance for redevelopment. It does not govern or limit the residence homestead exemption amount under Tax Code §11.13(b). No conflict.
quote: The legislature by general law may authorize cities, towns, and other taxing units to grant exemptions or other relief from ad valorem taxes on property located in a reinvestment zone for the purpose of encouraging development or redevelopment and improvement of the property.
_note: No conflict. The constitutional provision cited (art. VIII §1-g) governs reinvestment zone tax relief and TIF bonds, not residence homestead exemption limits. The relevant constitutional authority for homestead exemptions is art. VIII §1-b or §1-j.
